lever.description: eval_cholesky stores L as complex and runs its O(n^3) inner accumulation with complex_mul/complex_conj/complex_add (~8 flops/term) even for real inputs, where L is provably real (a real SPD matrix factors to a real lower-triangular L). For a real dtype, accumulate the inner sum with real f64 mul+add (2 flops/term) and pass (acc, 0.0) to the unchanged downstream logic. The diagonal positive-definite check, sqrt, and off-diagonal complex_div are untouched, so L is bit-for-bit identical. Complex/Hermitian inputs keep the complex path.

benchmarks.method: rch Criterion, eval_primitive(Cholesky, 128x128 real SPD); same-worker back-to-back; baseline = complex inner loop, optimized = real inner loop

after.tool: rch exec -- cargo bench -p fj-lax --bench lax_baseline -- 'linalg/cholesky_128x128_f64'

benchmarks.note: Non-overlapping CIs. The win is the inner-loop flop reduction; the O(n^2) complex_div/sqrt steps are unchanged (kept for bit-parity), bounding the speedup.

score.note: Algorithmic flop reduction (eliminates guaranteed-zero complex arithmetic). Real, production (Cholesky), bit-exact.

isomorphism_proof.floating_point: For real operands complex_mul((a,0), complex_conj((b,0))).re = a*b - 0*(-0) = a*b and the accumulated imaginary part is 0.0; complex_add of (re,0) terms keeps im 0.0. The real inner loop accumulates the same sum(a*b) in the same ascending-k order, then constructs (acc, 0.0). complex_sub/sqrt/complex_div downstream consume identical (re, 0.0) values, producing bit-identical L.
isomorphism_proof.ordering: Inner accumulation order over k unchanged.
isomorphism_proof.rng: No RNG state is read or written.
isomorphism_proof.errors: The not-positive-definite check (diag <= 0 or |imag| > 1e-10) is unchanged and still runs on the same diag_val.
isomorphism_proof.bit_identity_proof: New test cholesky_real_fast_path_bit_identical_to_complex_path factors the same 6x6 real SPD matrix as F64 (real path) and as Complex128 (complex path) and asserts f64::to_bits() equality of the factor real parts and that the complex path's imaginary parts are exactly zero. Existing cholesky SPD/roundtrip unit tests and the linalg oracle conformance plus 1010/1010 fj-lax tests pass.
